rationale: Ground truth here is thin (mostly an LGTM/approval plus one inline nit asking whether reduce_scatter test coverage needs strengthening); neither candidate directly hits that specific ask, so recall is low for both. X reaches a well-argued APPROVE verdict backed by genuine deep verification (checking actual vLLM DPMetadata/GroupCoordinator semantics) matching the real outcome, but produces only one concrete actionable comment. Y stays tightly diff-grounded and surfaces five specific file/line findings (test-order fragility, untested cfg=1 and world_size<=1 branches, undocumented DPMetadata interface, unguarded cpu_group access) that are individually minor but concrete, evidence-cited, and actionable, giving it an edge on precision and actionability despite similarly weak recall.
